mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production growth trend reflects real output momentum that feeds into corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% represents restrictive monetary policy that drains market liquidity and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint labor market deterioration defined as a rising unemployment rate and falling payroll employment over a three-month window signals weakening aggregate income and consumption, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months and PAYEMS falls > 200k over 3 months
failure_condition: When the joint trigger is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, accelerating housing starts reflect resurgent construction activity and multiplier-driven demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery regimes, rising HOUST is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that translates into lower corporate revenues and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS 3-month change is negative, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market that generates wage-push cost pressures and compresses profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate above 4% and declining industrial production creates a restrictive monetary environment with contracting output, which drives sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% and INDPRO declines over 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2% annualized signals robust aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and lifts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds 2%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of release months.
